calendar.matters:
PETITION Petition For Order Revoking Authority Of Attorney In Fact Or, In The Alternative, For Reasonable Visitation Rights
Off Calendar Apr-13-2009 Off Calendar

PETITION Petition For Order Revoking Authority Of Attorney In Fact Or, In The Alternative, For Reasonable Visitation Rights
Off Calendar Mar-16-2009 Continued To Apr-13-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION Petition For Order Revoking Authority Of Attorney In Fact Or, In The Alternative, For Reasonable Visitation Rights
Off Calendar Dec-08-2008 Continued To Mar-16-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Boessenecker for and with James Foppiano (Petitioner). Don Dowling for Joan Foppiano (Respondent). Andrea Girvin (alternate agent for healthcare) also present.

The parties presented argument to support their positions regarding the allegations of the breach of visitation, presentation of documents to the Senior Foppiano, and the repayment of the loans. Upon the conclusion of argument, the Court stated that the July 23, 2008 order was to remain in place. As to the issue regarding off-site visits, it is not medically in the best interest of the Senior Foppiano. Counsel are to work out a holiday visitation schedule. The Court admonished parties that they are not to go up to the room if someone else is already visiting, and parties are to give notice of hospitalization of either parent. Mr. Dowling is to prepare the order and is to submit it to Mr. Boessenecker for approval. The matter was continued to March 16, 2009 for further status.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: J. Alameda./R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

PETITION Petition For Order Revoking Authority Of Attorney In Fact Or, In The Alternative, For Reasonable Visitation Rights
Off Calendar Nov-17-2008 Continued To Dec-08-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION Petition For Order Revoking Authority Of Attorney In Fact Or, In The Alternative, For Reasonable Visitation Rights
Off Calendar Sep-29-2008 Continued To Nov-17-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Don Dowling for and with Joan Foppiano. Andrea Girth (Interested Party). John Boessenecker for and with James Foppiano.

The Court inquired as to the status of mediation. Mr. Boessenecker stated that things were not going well with mediation, that he had talked with counsel and agreed that they needed the doctor's reports from the physicians, stated that his client would like to take his parents out to lunch on short trips, and stated that he would like more than two individuals at a time allowed at the visits; including the adult children. Mr. Dowling stated that he wanted the doctors to give updated reports regarding the visits, noted there had been issues between the siblings/family at recent visits, and requested a modified visitation order removing Georganne for the reasons set forth on the record. The Court stated it wanted to see the doctor's reports, that the July 24, 2008, order was to remain in place, and wanted the doctor's reports to address the issue of going out to lunch and the issue of visits with more than two persons at one time. Mr. Dowling requested that James Foppiano provide twenty-four hour notice prior to a visit. Mr. Boessenecker suggested that the parties could avoid contact at any future visits by simply reviewing the register to see if a visit was already taking place; the later party could then wait for the first visit to conclude. The Court continued the matter to November 17, 2008 for status on the medical reports; and requested that the medical reports be submitted mid-week prior to the hearing date.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda/ R.Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

PETITION Petition For Order Revoking Authority Of Attorney In Fact Or, In The Alternative, For Reasonable Visitation Rights
Off Calendar Sep-08-2008 Continued To Sep-29-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION Petition For Order Revoking Authority Of Attorney In Fact Or, In The Alternative, For Reasonable Visitation Rights
Off Calendar Aug-12-2008 Continued To Sep-08-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION Petition For Order Revoking Authority Of Attorney In Fact Or, In The Alternative, For Reasonable Visitation Rights
Off Calendar Jul-21-2008 Continued To Aug-12-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION Petition For Order Revoking Authority Of Attorney In Fact Or, In The Alternative, For Reasonable Visitation Rights
Off Calendar May-19-2008 Continued To Jul-21-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Boessenecker for and with Petitioner, James Foppiano, Mrs. Georganne Foppino (wife) is also present in court; Don Dowling for and with Respondent/attorney-in- fact, Joan Foppina.

Parties reached a settlement agreement as follows: James and Georganne Foppino will allowed to visit their Parents (Robert and Margaret Foppiano) at the residential care facility with their three children, limited to two people visit at a time. Any future off-site visit to be determine by the doctor, and no document of any legal impact will be signed. Parties also agreed to keep the matter on calendar for 60 days to monitor the visitation process.
Parties agreed to terms on the record. The Court noted that the agreement was voluntary made by the parties and continued the matter to July 21, 2008.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha. Bailiff: Deputy Guitron.
